DISCOVERY 302, COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: PLAINTIFF REGINA CARLIN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES. MOTION IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST COUNSEL KATRIN CUSACK IN THE AMOUNT OF $3500.00. THE MOTION AS TO COMPELLING OR DEEMING ADMITTED THE REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE WAS SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE WITH THE CCP AND THE PRIOR COURT ORDER. MOTION AS TO THE FORM INTERROGATORIES IS DENIED AS TO INTERROGATORY NO. 17.1 AND GRANTED AS TO INTERROGATORIES 12.4 AND 14.2. THE PRIOR COURT ORDER REQUIRED A STATEMENT OF DILIGENCE AND EFFORTS OF SEARCH WHEN NO ANSWER WAS KNOWN. NO SUCH STATEMENT WAS PROVIDED. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. A DECLARATION IS NOT A PROPER RESPONSE AND THE PRIOR WRITTEN RESPONSES ARE NOT COMPLIANT WITH CCP SECTIONS 2031.210-2031.230. THERE IS NOT STATEMENT DELINEATING WHETHER PRODUCTION WILL BE MADE OR IF NOT, THE REASON FOR THE INABILITY. FINALLY, THE LACK OF VERIFICATIONS FOR THE ORIGINAL RESPONSES AND THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES ARE IN VIOLATION OF THE CCP AND THE PRIOR COURT ORDER. COMPLIANCE BY MR. TOBIN AND PAYMENT OF SANCTIONS BY COUNSEL, MS. CUSACK, IS ORDERED ON OR BEFORE 15 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. JUDGE PROTEM ELISABETH RILES, NOT REPORTED, =(302/HK)

Ntc Of Renewed Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses
Off Calendar Jan-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David M. Zeff (415-923-1380) of Law Offices of David M. Zeff for Regina M. Carlin, plaintiff and moving party / Katrin M. Cusack (925-820-2300) of Law Offices of Katrin M. Cusack for Patrick Tobin, defendant and opposing party

ARGUED - Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:
PLAINTIFF REGINA CARLIN Ntc Of Renewed Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses. Motion is denied in part and granted in part. Sanctions are awarded against counsel Katrin Cusack in the amount of $3500.00. The motion as to compelling or deeming admitted the Request for Admissions is denied. The Court finds that there was substantial compliance with the CCP and the prior Court order. Motion as to the Form Interrogatories is denied as to Interrogatory No. 17.1 and granted as to Interrogatories 12.4 and 14.2. The prior Court order required a statement of diligence and efforts of search when no answer was known. No such statement was provided. Motion is granted as to the Requests for Production of Documents. A declaration is not a proper response and the prior written responses are not compliant with CCP sections 2031.210-2031.230. There is not statement delineating whether production will be made or if not, the reason for the inability. Finally, the lack of verifications for the original responses and the supplemental responses are in violation of the CCP and the prior court order. Compliance by Mr. Tobin and payment of sanctions by counsel, Ms. Cusack, is ordered on or before 15 days of service of Notice of Entry of Order. JUDGE PROTEM ELISABETH RILES, NOT REPORTED, =(302/JPT)
